extraction.one_line_summary: WILLIAMS Stephen won uphill sprint finish after peloton caught break on final climb, with UAE tandem VLASOV/HIRSCHI driving pace before 26-year-old Welsh rider outsprinted SCHACHMANN and KRON
tactical_patterns: VLASOV Alexander and HIRSCHI Marc UAE tandem briefly separated peloton on final climb, revealing coordinated dual-protected leadership structure
Break caught on final categorized climb rather than descent indicates teams prioritized controlled selective peloton over risky chase
WILLIAMS Stephen positioned on left in sprint chaos while favorites chose center/right, suggesting underestimation created space for surprise winner

rider_signals.WILLIAMS Stephen: positive: first World Tour win at 26 suggests late development curve, converted positioning advantage when favorites marked each other
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: positive: attacked with HIRSCHI showing UAE comfortable deploying dual cards on lumpy finishes despite neither finishing top-3
rider_signals.SCHACHMANN Max: neutral: second place but failed to cover left side where winner emerged, prioritized wrong positioning in final 200m
rider_signals.KRON Andreas: positive: third as best young rider signals climbing punch adequate for lumpy one-week stages despite sprint limitations
rider_signals.HIRSCHI Marc: positive: shared attacking duties with VLASOV before leadout work, demonstrating tactical flexibility beyond pure finishing role
rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: negative: dropped on final climb of 180km lumpy stage indicates form deficit even on terrain theoretically suited to his characteristics
rider_signals.MARTINEZ Daniel: negative: distanced alongside PINOT on modest gradient suggests insufficient racing load or recovery issues rather than pure form
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: eliminated before final circuit lap confirms complete inability to contest even lumpy sprint stages at this career stage
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: negative: dropped with SAGAN indicates similar decline trajectory, cannot survive selective pace on categorized climbs before sprint
